workflow: Loop detects td review mode before delegated review and emits a mode-correct approve/close path; reviewer is never asked for --record-only in a trusted-mode DB

steps.label: detect-trusted
steps.result: resolved mode=trusted (source=default), matching the validation DB; record_only_supported=false

steps.label: reviewer-command-avoids-record-only
steps.action: assert '--record-only' not in reviewer_command and '--reason' in reviewer_command
steps.result: reviewer_command='td approve td-afe55b --reason "<review summary>"' — the exact recovery path the validation run had to fall back to

steps.label: delegated-branch
steps.result: reviewer_command uses --record-only; orchestrator closes with 'using recorded approval'

steps.label: config-mismatch-warning
steps.result: emits WARNING 'config expects delegated but resolved is trusted'; exit code 1

steps.label: td-unavailable-fallback
steps.result: falls back to trusted + warning; does not crash

steps.label: prose-fix
steps.action: grep SKILL.md 'Agent Selection' + 'Detect the review mode first'
steps.result: no longer unconditionally asks for --record-only; recipe lists trusted (default) path first

notes: UAT is CLI/functional: this issue changes a Codex skill's guidance + adds a no-deps helper script. There is no browser/UI workflow, login, or external service to verify; the failure mode (validation log line 27: '--record-only requires review_policy_mode=delegated') is reproduced and prevented deterministically.
install.sh uses cp -R of the repo, so scripts/review_close_path.py ships with the skill; executable bit set, consistent with record_human_uat.py and validate_config.py.
